---
title: FreeCampus Python
description: A beginner-first Python course from zero programming experience to practical projects.
---

::: {.hero}
# Python from Zero to Hero

This course is for learners who are new to programming. It starts in Google
Colab, where students can run Python in a browser, then gradually introduces
local Python, VS Code, Jupyter, debugging, testing, scientific computing, data
science, and introductory AI libraries.
:::

## Start here

- Read the [lesson index](lessons/index.qmd) to understand the full path.
- Begin with [Start Here](lessons/00-start-here.qmd) if programming is new to
  you.
- Use [Google Colab Quickstart](lessons/01-colab-quickstart.qmd) if you want to
  practice immediately without installing anything.
- Keep [Debugging and Error Messages](lessons/10-debugging-errors.qmd) nearby
  whenever Python prints red text.

## Course promise

The course teaches programming as a learnable process:

1. predict what code will do;
2. run it;
3. explain the result in everyday language;
4. change one small thing;
5. debug using clues instead of guessing.

## Tools used

- [Google Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/) for no-install practice.
- [Python](https://www.python.org/downloads/) for local programming.
- [VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/) for project work.
- [Jupyter](https://jupyter.org/) for notebooks and data exploration.
- `ruff`, `pytest`, and type hints for code quality.
- NumPy, pandas, Matplotlib, scikit-learn, and introductory AI libraries for
  applied work.
